overview: The Moses Text Filter processes InlineText files used by the [Moses MT system](http://www.statmt.org/moses/). Each line in a Moses InlineText file is read as a separate text unit containing translatable text with inline codes represented by XLIFF-like elements (`<g>`, `<x>`, `<bx>`, `<ex>`) and line-breaks represented by `<lb/>`. Moses InlineText files are typically created from other document formats using the Moses InlineText Extraction Step.
processingNotes: **Input encoding**: Uses BOM if present; otherwise defaults to **UTF-8** regardless of any specified default encoding.
**Line-breaks**: Output preserves the same line-break type as the original input.
Reading a Moses InlineText file may yield **more text units** than the original document, because each segment of a multi-segment text unit becomes a separate entry in the Moses file.
To map Moses entries back to their original text units, process both the original file and the Moses file together (as done by the Moses InlineText Leveraging Step).

examples.title: Moses InlineText file format
examples.description: A Moses InlineText file with four text units. Each line is a separate entry. Inline codes use XLIFF-like elements (`<g>`, `<x>`, `<bx>`, `<ex>`) and line-breaks use `<lb/>`.
examples.input:
```
Text in the first entry.
Text of the second entry<lb/>which spans<lb/>several lines
Third entry.
Fourth entry with <g id="1">bold words</g> and some code:<x id="2"/>
```
